source.title: Accident Investigation Report - MC 807 Platform A - Hand Laceration

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 807

actions_taken: Onsite medic provided first aid treatment
IP evacuated to onshore medical facility in Morgan City, LA
IP received sutures and released back to duty
IP returned to platform on 26 February 2023
event.summary: On 24 February 2023 at approximately 1115 hours, a Mechanic employed by Helmerich & Payne was attempting to install a hydraulic line on the Pipe Handling Machine (PHM) at Shell's Mars Tension Leg Platform. When the combination wrench being used was determined to be too long to access the fitting, the IP decided to modify the wrench by cutting it shorter with a manual hack saw. The wrench was placed in a vise, and as the IP cut through the final section, his weight shifted causing his hand to come down onto the freshly cut sharp edge, resulting in a laceration between the ring and little finger. The IP was treated onsite, then evacuated for sutures at an onshore medical facility. Investigation found inadequate pre-job planning, lack of supervision, and failure to obtain proper tools as probable causes.
recommendations: The BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time.

threats.name: Improper tool modification creating sharp edges
Inadequate pre-job planning and tool selection
Lack of supervision during non-standard work

controls.name: Pre-job safety and operation meeting
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper tools and procedures identified before work begins
controls.lod_basis: First line administrative control to prevent improper work execution

supporting_text: Pre-job safety and operation meeting proves to be inadequate by not ensuring the correct tools for the job task were gathered
Supervision – No or inadequate pre-job safety and operation meeting: IP did not discuss cutting the wrench with coworker or supervisor

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) with stop work authority
controls.barrier_role: Require work stoppage when new exposures recognized
controls.lod_basis: First line administrative control requiring reassessment when conditions change

supporting_text: H&P's Job Safety Analysis (JSA) states at the top, 'If during the task new exposures are recognized which are not covered in the JSA, STOP the job, reassess the risks, and revise the JSA.'

Once it was determined that a shorter wrench was needed, the job should have been stopped, the risks should have been reassessed, and a revision to the JSA should have been made with the supervisor's approval

controls.name: Proper tool selection and availability
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct tools gathered before job execution
controls.lod_basis: First line control to ensure proper equipment available

supporting_text: Supervision – Not providing adequate tools or equipment for tasks: JSA lists 'Gather tools needed for job' as step 2, but the correct tool for the job task was not obtained
The IP made a decision to alter the wrench instead of attempting to locate a close radius hand tool
There was no discussion with the supervisor about modifying the wrench or obtaining the proper tool

controls.name: Supervisor oversight and approval for non-standard work
controls.barrier_role: Ensure supervisor approval before modifying tools or procedures
controls.lod_basis: Second line supervisory verification before non-standard activities

supporting_text: BSEE's investigation finds lack of supervision to be a probable cause leading up to the incident
There was no discussion with the supervisor about modifying the wrench or obtaining the proper tool
The IP did not discuss altering the combination wrench with the electrician coworker

controls.name: Cut resistant gloves
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of hand injuries from sharp edges
controls.lod_basis: First line personal protective equipment to mitigate injury severity

supporting_text: The IP was wearing gloves at the time of the incident; however, they were not cut resistant

controls.name: Onsite medical treatment and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical response to injuries
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to minimize injury consequences after event

supporting_text: An onsite medic provided first aid treatment to the IP, but determined further treatment was needed at an onshore medical facility
At 1556 hours, the IP was sent onshore to the heliport in Houma, LA
The IP received sutures at the medical facility and was released back to duty

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
